ongrid.title: On-Grid Solar System
ongrid.description: An on-grid (grid-tied) system stays connected to the public electricity grid. Any excess solar power you generate is exported back to the grid through net metering, and you draw from the grid at night or on cloudy days.
points: Lowest cost system — no battery required
Net metering credits excess units to your bill
Best payback period among all solar types
Ideal for homes with reliable grid supply

offgrid.title: Off-Grid Solar System
offgrid.description: An off-grid system runs entirely independent of the public grid. Power is stored in batteries for use at night or during outages — ideal for remote sites or areas with unreliable electricity supply.
points: Complete independence from grid power cuts
Requires a larger battery bank — higher upfront cost
Best suited for remote areas or frequent outages
No net metering needed or available

hybrid.title: Hybrid Solar System
hybrid.description: A hybrid system combines grid connection with battery backup. You get net metering benefits during the day and automatic battery backup during outages — the most flexible option for Indian homes.
points: Works through grid outages, unlike pure on-grid
Still earns net metering credit for excess units
Moderate upfront cost — battery sized to your need
Most recommended for areas with frequent power cuts
